content.problemStatement:
A certain parallel universe has exactly NNN people living in it.

The iii-th of these NNN people claims that they are the parent of exactly AiA_iAi​ of these NNN people.

However, some of these people might be lying — the iii-th person may be either telling the truth (in which case they have exactly AiA_iAi​ children) or lying (in which case they can have any number of children).

It is known that each person has **at most one** parent. Further, as one would expect, it is **not allowed** for a person's child to also be their ancestor.

What is the **maximum** possible number of truth-tellers in this universe?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of people.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of people that can be telling the truth.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Both people can be telling the truth, with the first person being the parent of the second.
Test case 222: If both people were telling the truth, they would be each others' parents — and that is not allowed. Hence, at most one of them can be telling the truth.
Test case 333: The first and third people cannot be telling the truth at the same time. However, it is possible for the first and second people to be truthful — person 111 can be the parent of person 222 and person 333. Hence, the answer is 222.
Test case 444: There are several ways to pick 222 people to be telling the truth — for example,

The parent of person 222 and person 333 is person 111
The parent of person 111 and person 555 is person 444

in which case persons number 111 and 444 would be telling the truth.
However, it can be shown that it is impossible for any three of them to be simultaneously telling the truth.
